what_it_does: Defines a 24x24 SVG adjustments icon: a rounded square frame containing three horizontal slider lines with circular handles.
observations: Uses a 0 0 24 24 viewBox with explicit 24px width and height.
Sets fill to none at the SVG level and stroke to currentColor.
Draws an outer rounded rectangle from x=3 y=3 with width and height 18 and rx=2.
Draws three horizontal paths at y=8, y=12, and y=16 from x=7 to x=17.
Places three circular slider handles at x=10, x=14, and x=9 with radius 1.4.
Circle handles use fill="white" while retaining stroked outlines.
standouts: Framed adjustment controls rather than free-floating sliders.
Offset circular handles create a recognizable slider-control motif.
The icon inherits stroke color through currentColor.
conventions: Uses rounded stroke caps and joins.
Keeps the asset self-contained with no ids, classes, scripts, or external references.
Mixes theme-aware stroke color with hard-coded white handle fills.
concerns: Hard-coded white fills on the handles may not adapt to every background or theme context.
notes: A compact framed sliders icon for representing adjustments or settings controls.
questions: Is the white handle fill intentional across all themes and backgrounds?
Is the outer square frame part of the intended icon language for this control?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests an adjustments icon, and the SVG depicts slider-style adjustment controls.
intention_signals: It appears intended to communicate configurable adjustments through three horizontal slider tracks inside a small control-panel frame.
novelty: Combines slider controls with an enclosing rounded square frame and white-filled handles.
contribution: Contributes a currentColor-compatible adjustments glyph with a bounded panel shape, three slider rows, and visibly offset control handles.
summary_for_embedding: A 24x24 SVG line icon showing a rounded square panel with three horizontal adjustment sliders and circular handles.
intention_for_embedding: This icon was for representing settings, tuning, or adjustment controls in a compact design-system asset.
